heading: Commissioner of Internal Revenue; other officials

Effective Date of 1998 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–206, title I, § 1102(f)112 Stat. 705July 22, 1998“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), the amendments made by this section [amending this section, sections 6212, 6323, 6343, 7611, and 7811 of this title, and , Government Organization and Employees] shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act [].section 5109 of Title 5July 22, 1998“(2).—Chief counselSection 7803(b)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as added by this section, shall take effect on the date that is 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act.“(3).—National taxpayer advocateNotwithstanding section 7803(c)(1)(B)(iv) of such Code, as added by this section, in appointing the first National Taxpayer Advocate after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of the Treasury—“(A)shall not appoint any individual who was an officer or employee of the Internal Revenue Service at any time during the 2-year period ending on the date of appointment; and“(B)need not consult with the Internal Revenue Service Oversight Board if the Oversight Board has not been appointed.“(4)Current officers.—“(A)In the case of an individual serving as Commissioner of Internal Revenue on the date of the enactment of this Act who was appointed to such position before such date, the 5-year term required by section 7803(a)(1) of such Code, as added by this section, shall begin as of the date of such appointment.“(B)Clauses (ii), (iii), and (iv) of section 7803(c)(1)(B) of such Code, as added by this section, shall not apply to the individual serving as Taxpayer Advocate on the date of the enactment of this Act.”

Coordination of IRS CIO and Chief Procurement Officer of the Internal Revenue Service, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–25, title II, § 2101(c)133 Stat. 1010July 1, 2019“(1).—In generalThe Chief Procurement Officer of the Internal Revenue Service shall—“(A)identify all significant IRS information technology acquisitions and provide written notification to the Internal Revenue Service Chief Information Officer (hereafter referred to in this subsection as the ‘IRS CIO’) of each such acquisition in advance of such acquisition, and“(B)regularly consult with the IRS CIO regarding acquisitions of information technology for the Internal Revenue Service, including meeting with the IRS CIO regarding such acquisitions upon request.“(2).—Significant irs information technology acquisitionsFor purposes of this subsection, the term ‘significant IRS information technology acquisitions’ means—“(A)any acquisition of information technology for the Internal Revenue Service in excess of $1 million; and“(B)such other acquisitions of information technology for the Internal Revenue Service (or categories of such acquisitions) as the IRS CIO, in consultation with the Chief Procurement Officer of the Internal Revenue Service, may identify.“(3).—ScopeTerms used in this subsection which are also used in section 7803(f) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by subsection (a)) shall have the same meaning as when used in such section.”

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: There shall be in the Department of the Treasury a Commissioner of Internal Revenue who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate. Such appointment shall be made from individuals who, among other qualifications, have a demonstrated ability in management.

children.heading: Term
children.label: B Term
children.text: The term of the Commissioner of Internal Revenue shall be a 5-year term, beginning with a term to commence on . Each subsequent term shall begin on the day after the date on which the previous term expires.November 13, 1997

children.heading: Vacancy
children.label: C Vacancy
children.text: Any individual appointed as Commissioner of Internal Revenue during a term as defined in subparagraph (B) shall be appointed for the remainder of that term.

children.heading: Removal
children.label: D Removal
children.text: The Commissioner may be removed at the will of the President.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: E Reappointment
children.text: The Commissioner may be appointed to serve more than one term.

children.label: A
children.text: administer, manage, conduct, direct, and supervise the execution and application of the internal revenue laws or related statutes and tax conventions to which the United States is a party; and

children.label: B
children.text: recommend to the President a candidate for appointment as Chief Counsel for the Internal Revenue Service when a vacancy occurs, and recommend to the President the removal of such Chief Counsel.

children.heading: Execution of duties in accord with taxpayer rights
children.label: 3 Execution of duties in accord with taxpayer rights
A
children.text: the right to be informed,

children.label: B
children.text: the right to quality service,

children.label: C
children.text: the right to pay no more than the correct amount of tax,

children.label: D
children.text: the right to challenge the position of the Internal Revenue Service and be heard,

children.label: E
children.text: the right to appeal a decision of the Internal Revenue Service in an independent forum,

children.label: F
children.text: the right to finality,

children.label: G
children.text: the right to privacy,

children.label: H
children.text: the right to confidentiality,

children.label: I
children.text: the right to retain representation, and

children.label: J
children.text: the right to a fair and just tax system.

children.heading: Consultation with Board
children.label: 4 Consultation with Board
children.text: The Commissioner shall consult with the Oversight Board on all matters set forth in paragraphs (2) and (3) (other than paragraph (3)(A)) of section 7802(d).

children.heading: Chief Counsel for the Internal Revenue Service
children.label: b Chief Counsel for the Internal Revenue Service

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: There shall be in the Department of the Treasury a Chief Counsel for the Internal Revenue Service who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the consent of the Senate.

children.label: A
children.text: to be legal advisor to the Commissioner and the Commissioner’s officers and employees;

children.label: B
children.text: to furnish legal opinions for the preparation and review of rulings and memoranda of technical advice;

children.label: C
children.text: to prepare, review, and assist in the preparation of proposed legislation, treaties, regulations, and Executive orders relating to laws which affect the Internal Revenue Service;

children.label: D
children.text: to represent the Commissioner in cases before the Tax Court; and

children.label: E
children.text: to determine which civil actions should be litigated under the laws relating to the Internal Revenue Service and prepare recommendations for the Department of Justice regarding the commencement of such actions.

children.heading: Persons to whom Chief Counsel reports
children.label: 3 Persons to whom Chief Counsel reports
i
children.text: legal advice or interpretation of the tax law not relating solely to tax policy;

children.label: ii
children.text: tax litigation; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Chief Counsel shall report to the General Counsel with respect to legal advice or interpretation of the tax law relating solely to tax policy.

children.heading: Chief Counsel personnel
children.label: 4 Chief Counsel personnel
children.text: All personnel in the Office of Chief Counsel shall report to the Chief Counsel.

children.heading: Office of the Taxpayer Advocate
children.label: c Office of the Taxpayer Advocate

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: There is established in the Internal Revenue Service an office to be known as the “Office of the Taxpayer Advocate”.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Office of the Taxpayer Advocate shall be under the supervision and direction of an official to be known as the “National Taxpayer Advocate”. The National Taxpayer Advocate shall report directly to the Commissioner of Internal Revenue and shall be entitled to compensation at the same rate as the highest rate of basic pay established for the Senior Executive Service under , United States Code.section 5382 of title 5

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: ii Appointment
children.text: The National Taxpayer Advocate shall be appointed by the Secretary of the Treasury after consultation with the Commissioner of Internal Revenue and the Oversight Board and without regard to the provisions of title 5, United States Code, relating to appointments in the competitive service or the Senior Executive Service.

children.label: I
children.text: a background in customer service as well as tax law; and

children.label: II
children.text: experience in representing individual taxpayers.

children.heading: Restriction on employment
children.label: iv Restriction on employment
children.text: An individual may be appointed as the National Taxpayer Advocate only if such individual was not an officer or employee of the Internal Revenue Service during the 2-year period ending with such appointment and such individual agrees not to accept any employment with the Internal Revenue Service for at least 5 years after ceasing to be the National Taxpayer Advocate. Service as an officer or employee of the Office of the Taxpayer Advocate shall not be taken into account in applying this clause.

children.label: i
children.text: assist taxpayers in resolving problems with the Internal Revenue Service;

children.label: ii
children.text: identify areas in which taxpayers have problems in dealings with the Internal Revenue Service;

children.label: iii
children.text: to the extent possible, propose changes in the administrative practices of the Internal Revenue Service to mitigate problems identified under clause (ii); and

children.label: iv
children.text: identify potential legislative changes which may be appropriate to mitigate such problems.

children.heading: Objectives
children.label: i Objectives
children.text: Not later than June 30 of each calendar year, the National Taxpayer Advocate shall report to the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate on the objectives of the Office of the Taxpayer Advocate for the fiscal year beginning in such calendar year. Any such report shall contain full and substantive analysis, in addition to statistical information.

children.label: I
children.text: identify the initiatives the Office of the Taxpayer Advocate has taken on improving taxpayer services and Internal Revenue Service responsiveness;

children.label: II
children.text: contain recommendations received from individuals with the authority to issue Taxpayer Assistance Orders under section 7811;

children.label: III
children.text: contain a summary of the 10 most serious problems encountered by taxpayers, including a description of the nature of such problems;

children.label: IV
children.text: contain an inventory of the items described in subclauses (I), (II), and (III) for which action has been taken and the result of such action;

children.label: V
children.text: contain an inventory of the items described in subclauses (I), (II), and (III) for which action remains to be completed and the period during which each item has remained on such inventory;

children.label: VI
children.text: contain an inventory of the items described in subclauses (I), (II), and (III) for which no action has been taken, the period during which each item has remained on such inventory, the reasons for the inaction, and identify any Internal Revenue Service official who is responsible for such inaction;

children.label: VII
children.text: identify any Taxpayer Assistance Order which was not honored by the Internal Revenue Service in a timely manner, as specified under section 7811(b);

children.label: VIII
children.text: identify any Taxpayer Advocate Directive which was not honored by the Internal Revenue Service in a timely manner, as specified under paragraph (5);

children.label: IX
children.text: contain recommendations for such administrative and legislative action as may be appropriate to resolve problems encountered by taxpayers;

children.label: X
children.text: identify areas of the tax law that impose significant compliance burdens on taxpayers or the Internal Revenue Service, including specific recommendations for remedying these problems;

children.label: XI
children.text: identify the 10 most litigated issues for each category of taxpayers, including recommendations for mitigating such disputes;

children.label: XII
children.text: with respect to any statistical information included in such report, include a statement of whether such statistical information was reviewed or provided by the Secretary under section 6108(d) and, if so, whether the Secretary determined such information to be statistically valid and based on sound statistical methodology; and

children.label: XIII
children.text: include such other information as the National Taxpayer Advocate may deem advisable.

children.heading: Report to be submitted directly
children.label: iii Report to be submitted directly
children.text: Each report required under this subparagraph shall be provided directly to the committees described in clause (i) without any prior review or comment from the Commissioner, the Secretary of the Treasury, the Oversight Board, any other officer or employee of the Department of the Treasury, or the Office of Management and Budget. The preceding sentence shall not apply with respect to statistical information provided to the Secretary for review, or received from the Secretary, under section 6108(d).

children.heading: Coordination with report of Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration
children.label: iv Coordination with report of Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration
children.text: To the extent that information required to be reported under clause (ii) is also required to be reported under paragraph (1) or (2) of subsection (d) by the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration, the National Taxpayer Advocate shall not contain such information in the report submitted under such clause.

children.label: i
children.text: monitor the coverage and geographic allocation of local offices of taxpayer advocates;

children.label: ii
children.text: develop guidance to be distributed to all Internal Revenue Service officers and employees outlining the criteria for referral of taxpayer inquiries to local offices of taxpayer advocates;

children.label: iii
children.text: ensure that the local telephone number for each local office of the taxpayer advocate is published and available to taxpayers served by the office; and

children.label: iv
children.text: in conjunction with the Commissioner, develop career paths for local taxpayer advocates choosing to make a career in the Office of the Taxpayer Advocate.

children.label: I
children.text: appoint local taxpayer advocates and make available at least 1 such advocate for each State; and

children.label: II
children.text: evaluate and take personnel actions (including dismissal) with respect to any employee of any local office of a taxpayer advocate described in subclause (I).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: ii Consultation
children.text: The National Taxpayer Advocate may consult with the appropriate supervisory personnel of the Internal Revenue Service in carrying out the National Taxpayer Advocate’s responsibilities under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Coordination with Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration
children.label: E Coordination with Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration
children.text: Before beginning any research or study, the National Taxpayer Advocate shall coordinate with the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration to ensure that the National Taxpayer Advocate does not duplicate any action that the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration has already undertaken or has a plan to undertake.

children.heading: Responsibilities of Commissioner
children.label: 3 Responsibilities of Commissioner
children.text: The Commissioner shall establish procedures requiring a formal response to all recommendations submitted to the Commissioner by the National Taxpayer Advocate within 3 months after submission to the Commissioner.

children.label: i
children.text: shall report to the National Taxpayer Advocate or delegate thereof;

children.label: ii
children.text: may consult with the appropriate supervisory personnel of the Internal Revenue Service regarding the daily operation of the local office of the taxpayer advocate;

children.label: iii
children.text: shall, at the initial meeting with any taxpayer seeking the assistance of a local office of the taxpayer advocate, notify such taxpayer that the taxpayer advocate offices operate independently of any other Internal Revenue Service office and report directly to Congress through the National Taxpayer Advocate; and

children.label: iv
children.text: may, at the taxpayer advocate’s discretion, not disclose to the Internal Revenue Service contact with, or information provided by, such taxpayer.

children.heading: Maintenance of independent communications
children.label: B Maintenance of independent communications
children.text: Each local office of the taxpayer advocate shall maintain a separate phone, facsimile, and other electronic communication access, and a separate post office address.

children.label: A
children.text: the Commissioner or a Deputy Commissioner shall modify, rescind, or ensure compliance with such directive not later than 90 days after the issuance of such directive, and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any directive which is modified or rescinded by a Deputy Commissioner, the National Taxpayer Advocate may (not later than 90 days after such modification or rescission) appeal to the Commissioner, and the Commissioner shall (not later than 90 days after such appeal is made) ensure compliance with such directive as issued by the National Taxpayer Advocate or provide the National Taxpayer Advocate with the reasons for any modification or rescission made or upheld by the Commissioner pursuant to such appeal.

children.heading: Additional duties of the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration
children.label: d Additional duties of the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration
i
children.text: restrictions under section 1204 of the Internal Revenue Service Restructuring and Reform Act of 1998 on the use of enforcement statistics to evaluate Internal Revenue Service employees;

children.label: ii
children.text: restrictions under section 7521 on directly contacting taxpayers who have indicated that they prefer their representatives be contacted;

children.label: iii
children.text: required procedures under section 6320 upon the filing of a notice of a lien;

children.label: iv
children.text: required procedures under subchapter D of chapter 64 for seizure of property for collection of taxes, including required procedures under section 6330 regarding levies; and

children.label: v
children.text: restrictions under section 3707 of the Internal Revenue Service Restructuring and Reform Act of 1998 on designation of taxpayers;

children.label: B
children.text: a review and a certification of whether or not the Secretary is complying with the requirements of section 6103(e)(8) to disclose information to an individual filing a joint return on collection activity involving the other individual filing the return;

children.label: C
children.text: information regarding extensions of the statute of limitations for assessment and collection of tax under section 6501 and the provision of notice to taxpayers regarding requests for such extension;

children.label: D
children.text: an evaluation of the adequacy and security of the technology of the Internal Revenue Service;

children.label: E
children.text: any termination or mitigation under section 1203 of the Internal Revenue Service Restructuring and Reform Act of 1998;

children.label: F
children.text: information regarding improper denial of requests for information from the Internal Revenue Service identified under paragraph (3)(A); and

children.label: i
children.text: a summary of such actions initiated since the date of the last report; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a summary of any judgments or awards granted as a result of such actions.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of taxpayer complaints during the reporting period;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of employee misconduct and taxpayer abuse allegations received by the Internal Revenue Service or the Inspector General during the period from taxpayers, Internal Revenue Service employees, and other sources;

children.label: iii
children.text: a summary of the status of such complaints and allegations; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a summary of the disposition of such complaints and allegations, including the outcome of any Department of Justice action and any monies paid as a settlement of such complaints and allegations.

children.label: B
children.text: Clauses (iii) and (iv) of subparagraph (A) shall only apply to complaints and allegations of serious employee misconduct.

children.label: A
children.text: conduct periodic audits of a statistically valid sample of the total number of determinations made by the Internal Revenue Service to deny written requests to disclose information to taxpayers on the basis of or , United States Code;section 6103 of this titlesection 552(b)(7) of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: establish and maintain a toll-free telephone number for taxpayers to use to confidentially register complaints of misconduct by Internal Revenue Service employees and incorporate the telephone number in the statement required by section 6227 of the Omnibus Taxpayer Bill of Rights (Internal Revenue Service Publication No. 1); and

children.label: C
children.text: not later than , submit a written report to Congress on the implementation of section 6103(k)(10).December 31, 2010

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Internal Revenue Service an office to be known as the “Internal Revenue Service Independent Office of Appeals”.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Internal Revenue Service Independent Office of Appeals shall be under the supervision and direction of an official to be known as the “Chief of Appeals”. The Chief of Appeals shall report directly to the Commissioner of Internal Revenue and shall be entitled to compensation at the same rate as the highest rate of basic pay established for the Senior Executive Service under , United States Code.section 5382 of title 5

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: B Appointment
children.text: The Chief of Appeals shall be appointed by the Commissioner of Internal Revenue without regard to the provisions of title 5, United States Code, relating to appointments in the competitive service or the Senior Executive Service.

children.label: i
children.text: administration of, and compliance with, Federal tax laws,

children.label: ii
children.text: a broad range of compliance cases, and

children.label: iii
children.text: management of large service organizations.

children.label: A
children.text: is fair and impartial to both the Government and the taxpayer,

children.label: B
children.text: promotes a consistent application and interpretation of, and voluntary compliance with, the Federal tax laws, and

children.label: C
children.text: enhances public confidence in the integrity and efficiency of the Internal Revenue Service.

children.heading: Right of appeal
children.label: 4 Right of appeal
children.text: The resolution process described in paragraph (3) shall be generally available to all taxpayers.

children.heading: Limitation on designation of cases as not eligible for referral to Independent Office of Appeals
children.label: 5 Limitation on designation of cases as not eligible for referral to Independent Office of Appeals
i
children.text: provides a detailed description of the facts involved, the basis for the decision to deny the request, and a detailed explanation of how the basis of such decision applies to such facts, and

children.label: ii
children.text: describes the procedures prescribed under subparagraph (C) for protesting the decision to deny the request.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: B Report to Congress
children.text: The Commissioner of Internal Revenue shall submit a written report to Congress on an annual basis which includes the number of requests described in subparagraph (A) which were denied and the reasons (described by category) that such requests were denied.

children.heading: Procedures for protesting denial of request
children.label: C Procedures for protesting denial of request
children.text: The Commissioner of Internal Revenue shall prescribe procedures for protesting to the Commissioner of Internal Revenue a denial of a request described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Not applicable to frivolous positions
children.label: D Not applicable to frivolous positions
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to a request for referral to the Internal Revenue Service Independent Office of Appeals which is denied on the basis that the issue involved is a frivolous position (within the meaning of section 6702(c)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: All personnel in the Internal Revenue Service Independent Office of Appeals shall report to the Chief of Appeals.

children.heading: Access to staff of Office of the Chief Counsel
children.label: B Access to staff of Office of the Chief Counsel
children.text: The Chief of Appeals shall have authority to obtain legal assistance and advice from the staff of the Office of the Chief Counsel. The Chief Counsel shall ensure, to the extent practicable, that such assistance and advice is provided by staff of the Office of the Chief Counsel who were not involved in the case with respect to which such assistance and advice is sought and who are not involved in preparing such case for litigation.

children.heading: Access to case files1See Effective Date of 2019 Amendment note below.1
children.label: 7 Access to case files1See Effective Date of 2019 Amendment note below.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In any case in which a conference with the Internal Revenue Service Independent Office of Appeals has been scheduled upon request of a specified taxpayer, the Chief of Appeals shall ensure that such taxpayer is provided access to the nonprivileged portions of the case file on record regarding the disputed issues (other than documents provided by the taxpayer to the Internal Revenue Service) not later than 10 days before the date of such conference.

children.heading: Taxpayer election to expedite conference
children.label: B Taxpayer election to expedite conference
children.text: If the taxpayer so elects, subparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting “the date of such conference” for “10 days before the date of such conference”.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of any taxpayer who is a natural person, a taxpayer whose adjusted gross income does not exceed $400,000 for the taxable year to which the dispute relates, and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any other taxpayer, a taxpayer whose gross receipts do not exceed $5 million for the taxable year to which the dispute relates.

children.heading: Aggregation rule
children.label: ii Aggregation rule
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 448(c)(2) shall apply for purposes of clause (i)(II).

children.heading: Internal Revenue Service Chief Information Officer
children.label: f Internal Revenue Service Chief Information Officer

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There shall be in the Internal Revenue Service an Internal Revenue Service Chief Information Officer (hereafter referred to in this subsection as the “IRS CIO”) who shall be appointed by the Commissioner of Internal Revenue.

children.heading: Centralized responsibility for Internal Revenue Service information technology
children.label: 2 Centralized responsibility for Internal Revenue Service information technology
children.text: The Commissioner of Internal Revenue (and the Secretary) shall act through the IRS CIO with respect to all development, implementation, and maintenance of information technology for the Internal Revenue Service. Any reference in this subsection to the IRS CIO which directs the IRS CIO to take any action, or to assume any responsibility, shall be treated as a reference to the Commissioner of Internal Revenue acting through the IRS CIO.

children.label: A
children.text: be responsible for the development, implementation, and maintenance of information technology for the Internal Revenue Service,

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that the information technology of the Internal Revenue Service is secure and integrated,

children.label: C
children.text: maintain operational control of all information technology for the Internal Revenue Service,

children.label: D
children.text: be the principal advocate for the information technology needs of the Internal Revenue Service, and

children.label: i
children.text: the goals and requirements specified in subparagraphs (A) through (D), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the strategic plan developed under paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: include performance measurements of such technology and of the implementation of such plan,

children.label: ii
children.text: include a plan for an integrated enterprise architecture of the information technology of the Internal Revenue Service,

children.label: iii
children.text: include and take into account the resources needed to accomplish such plan,

children.label: iv
children.text: take into account planned major acquisitions of information technology by the Internal Revenue Service, and

children.label: v
children.text: align with the needs and strategic plan of the Internal Revenue Service.

children.heading: Plan updates
children.label: B Plan updates
children.text: The IRS CIO shall, not less frequently than annually, review and update the strategic plan under subparagraph (A) (including the plan for an integrated enterprise architecture described in subparagraph (A)(ii)) to take into account the development of new information technology and the needs of the Internal Revenue Service.

children.heading: Information technology
children.label: A Information technology
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “information technology” has the meaning given such term by , United States Code.section 11101 of title 40

children.label: i
children.text: the Office of the Taxpayer Advocate,

children.label: ii
children.text: the Criminal Investigation Division of the Internal Revenue Service, and

children.label: iii
children.text: except as otherwise provided by the Secretary with respect to information technology related to matters described in subsection (b)(3)(B), the Office of the Chief Counsel.
